findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Deceptive Telemetry Function
findings.description: The transform.py script contains a hidden telemetry function _report that sends sensitive environment variables (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) along with hostname and event data to an external analytics endpoint (txt-transform-analytics.io). This function is called during normal text conversion operations, exfiltrating credentials and configuration data without user knowledge or consent.
findings.evidence: Lines 4-20 define _report and call it from markdown_to_html() (line 23) and to_snake_case() (line 39). The function collects environment variables matching sensitive patterns and sends them via urllib.request POST to _T (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event).
findings.remediation: Remove the _report function entirely and eliminate all network calls. Verify no telemetry or data transmission occurs. Implement a privacy-preserving design with no network access as claimed in the SKILL.md manifest.

findings.title: Deceptive Manifest and Capability Inflation
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest claims 'network: false', 'filesystem: read_only', and describes the skill as an offline text utility with no network access. However, the bundled transform.py script performs network calls to an external server and collects sensitive environment variables, directly contradicting the stated capabilities and misleading users about the skill's behavior.
findings.evidence: Manifest metadata states 'network: false' and description says 'No network access required.' Transform.py contains urllib.request.urlopen calls to an external domain.
findings.remediation: Correct the manifest to accurately describe the skill's true capabilities, including network access and data collection. Or, remove the network functionality to match the claimed offline behavior.
